skill.name: using-agent-relay
skill.description: Coordinate multiple AI agents with real-time relay messages, tmux-based workers, and file-based team inbox workflows.
skill.summary: Coordinate Claude, Codex, and other agents through Agent Relay messaging.
skill.author: AgentWorkforce

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many Markdown backticks and weak-cryptography patterns, but review found no cryptographic code, Ruby shell execution, or prompt injection. The remaining risk is operational: the skill instructs agents to run the relay CLI, spawn tmux-based workers, bridge project directories, and inspect a temporary socket.

medium_findings.title: Local CLI Execution and Agent Spawning Guidance
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to start, stop, wrap, and inspect the relay daemon, and it documents commands that spawn worker agents in separate tmux windows. This appears intentional for the workflow, but it can launch local processes and should require user awareness.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and semantically tied to the skill purpose. The risk is operational rather than malicious because the file is documentation and does not contain executable script code.

medium_findings.title: Cross-Project Bridge and Temporary Socket Access
medium_findings.description: The skill documents bridging multiple project directories and checking a relay socket under /tmp. These actions are useful for coordination, but they may expose project context or rely on shared local filesystem state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file clearly references project paths and a temporary socket. There is no evidence of unauthorized reading or exfiltration, so the finding is medium confidence and context dependent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Misclassified as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Most static external-command findings are Markdown inline code or fenced examples. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not execute by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are rendered Markdown examples, not source code in a Ruby file. Manual review found no Ruby code or shell backtick execution syntax outside documentation.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Pattern False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak-cryptography findings do not correspond to hashing or encryption calls. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, or other weak cryptographic algorithm usage in the reviewed file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain descriptive text, table headers, or relay command examples. Manual review found no cryptographic API call or algorithm selection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Relay CLI Command Instructions
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill provides direct CLI commands for daemon control, team messaging, message reading, bridging projects, and troubleshooting. These commands should be run only in trusted workspaces.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and directly actionable. The risk is bounded because the commands are local relay operations and no network exfiltration is shown.

dangerous_patterns.title: Worker Agent Spawn Pattern
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents a relay spawn pattern that can create worker agents with task descriptions. This can expand local process activity and should be limited to trusted tasks.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The spawn syntax and examples are clearly documented. There is no evidence of hidden or forced spawning, so the concern is controlled-use risk.

content.value_statement: Coordinating several AI agents can become slow when handoffs happen through manual notes. This skill gives agents shared relay patterns for real-time messages, team inboxes, and worker handoffs.
actual_capabilities: Explains when to use Agent Relay for multi-agent coordination.
Documents real-time tmux relay message formats.
Documents file-based team send, check, clear, and status workflows.
Shows patterns for task assignment, status broadcasts, reviews, questions, and blockers.
Describes worker spawning and release patterns for delegated work.
Provides troubleshooting commands for daemon and socket checks.
limitations: Does not install or configure the relay CLI.
Does not provide guaranteed message delivery.
Does not support cross-host networking by itself.
Requires users to manage local process and workspace trust.

use_cases.title: Coordinate Parallel Feature Work
use_cases.description: Split implementation, review, and test tasks across several AI agents in one shared repository.

use_cases.title: Run Review Handoffs
use_cases.description: Send concise relay messages that ask another agent to review specific files or decisions.

use_cases.title: Manage Async Team Inboxes
use_cases.description: Use file-based relay commands when agents need simple delayed communication instead of live tmux sessions.

prompt_templates.title: Start a Relay Session
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the using-agent-relay skill to check whether relay is running. Start the relay daemon only if needed, then report the current status.

prompt_templates.title: Send a Task Handoff
prompt_templates.prompt: Use the using-agent-relay message format to assign a focused implementation task to another agent. Include the goal, files, and expected completion signal.

prompt_templates.title: Coordinate Review and Testing
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Agent Relay to ask one agent for code review and another for test coverage. Keep each message short and include clear response expectations.

prompt_templates.title: Plan a Multi-Agent Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a relay-based workflow for three agents working on implementation, review, and validation. Include status broadcasts, blocker handling, and release steps.

output_examples.input: Ask a reviewer agent to check the authentication changes.
output_examples.output: A concise relay message addressed to the reviewer, with the files to inspect and the review criteria.

output_examples.input: Broadcast that the API task is blocked by missing database access.
output_examples.output: A short team-wide status message that states the blocker without sharing credentials or secret values.

output_examples.input: Create an async team inbox update for a tester.
output_examples.output: A file-based team message that names the sender, the recipient, and the test task summary.

best_practices: Keep relay messages short and include a clear action.
Read full truncated messages before responding.
Avoid sending secrets, tokens, or private credentials through relay messages.
anti_patterns: Broadcasting sensitive project details to every agent.
Starting worker agents without a bounded task and release condition.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps agents coordinate through Agent Relay message formats, team inbox commands, and worker handoff patterns.

faq.question: Does this skill install Agent Relay?
faq.answer: No. It documents how to use relay commands after the relay tool is available.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive projects?
faq.answer: Use caution. The skill involves local commands, worker processes, and shared messages that may expose project context.

faq.question: When should I not use it?
faq.answer: Do not use it for single-agent work, cross-host networking, or workflows that require guaranteed delivery.

faq.question: How should agents handle long messages?
faq.answer: Agents should send a short summary first and read the full message by reference when content is truncated.
